No Matches
write_halos.c
Go to the documentation of this file.
1/*****************************************************************
2 * PINOCCHIO V5.1 *
3 * (PINpointing Orbit-Crossing Collapsed HIerarchical Objects) *
4 *****************************************************************
5
6 This code was written by
7 Pierluigi Monaco, Tom Theuns, Giuliano Taffoni, Marius Lepinzan,
8 Chiara Moretti, Luca Tornatore, David Goz, Tiago Castro
9 Copyright (C) 2025
10
11 github: https://github.com/pigimonaco/Pinocchio
12 web page: http://adlibitum.oats.inaf.it/monaco/pinocchio.html
13
14 This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify
15 it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
16 the Free Software Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or
17 (at your option) any later version.
18
19 This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
20 but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
21 MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the
22 GNU General Public License for more details.
23
24 You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
25 along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
26 Foundation, Inc., 59 Temple Place, Suite 330, Boston, MA 02111-1307 USA
27*/
28
29#include "pinocchio.h"
30
31#define FTOZ(A) (A > 0.0 ? A - 1 : A);
32
33int NSlices = 1;
34
35int compute_mf(int iout)
36{
37 /* computes the mass function of the groups and the analytic mass function */
38
39 int i, ibin;
40 char filename[LBLENGTH], lab1[10], lab2[10];
41 double amass, x, dm, a, a1, a2, a3, m, D, mx, r, massvar, sigma, ni;
42 FILE *file;
43
44 D = GrowingMode(outputs.z[iout], params.k_for_GM);
45
46 /* sets counters to zero */
47 for (i = 0; i < mf.NBIN; i++)
48 {
49 mf.ninbin_local[i] = 0;
50 mf.massinbin_local[i] = 0.0;
51 mf.ninbin[i] = 0;
52 mf.massinbin[i] = 0.0;
53 }
54
55 /* constructs the histograms */
56 for (i = FILAMENT + 1; i <= ngroups; i++)
57 if (groups[i].point >= 0 && groups[i].good && groups[i].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
58 {
59 amass = groups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass;
60 ibin = (int)((log10(amass) - mf.mmin) / DELTAM);
61 if (ibin < 0 || ibin >= mf.NBIN)
62 continue;
63 mf.ninbin_local[ibin]++;
64 mf.massinbin_local[ibin] += amass;
65 }
66
67 /* sums counters over all tasks */
68 MPI_Reduce(mf.ninbin_local, mf.ninbin, mf.NBIN, MPI_INT, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
69 MPI_Reduce(mf.massinbin_local, mf.massinbin, mf.NBIN, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
70
71 /* Writes result */
72 if (!ThisTask)
73 {
74 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.mf.out", outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag);
75 printf("[%s] Writing mass function into file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
76 file = fopen(filename, "w");
77
78 fprintf(file, "# Mass function for redshift %f\n", outputs.z[iout]);
79 if (params.OutputInH100)
80 {
81 strcpy(lab1, "/h");
82 strcpy(lab2, "h^4");
83 }
84 else
85 {
86 strcpy(lab1, "");
87 strcpy(lab2, "");
88 }
89
90 fprintf(file, "# 1) mass (Msun%s)\n", lab1);
91 fprintf(file, "# 2) n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
92 fprintf(file, "# 3) upper +1-sigma limit for n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
93 fprintf(file, "# 4) lower -1-sigma limit for n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
94 fprintf(file, "# 5) number of halos in the bin\n");
95 switch (params.AnalyticMassFunction)
96 {
97 case 0:
98 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Press & Schechter 1974\n");
99 break;
100 case 1:
101 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Sheth & Tormen 2001\n");
102 break;
103 case 2:
104 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Jenkins et al. 2001\n");
105 break;
106 case 3:
107 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Warren et al. 2006\n");
108 break;
109 case 4:
110 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Reed et al. 2007\n");
111 break;
112 case 5:
113 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Crocce et al. 2010\n");
114 break;
115 case 6:
116 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Tinker et al. 2010\n");
117 break;
118 case 7:
119 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Courtin et al. 2010\n");
120 break;
121 case 8:
122 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Angulo et al. 2012\n");
123 break;
124 case 9:
125 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Watson et al. 2013\n");
126 break;
127 case 10:
128 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Crocce et al. 2010, universal\n");
129 break;
130 default:
131 break;
132 }
133 fprintf(file, "#\n");
134
135 for (i = 0; i < mf.NBIN; i++)
136 {
137 x = mf.mmin + (i + 0.5) * DELTAM;
138 m = pow(10., x);
139 dm = params.ParticleMass *
140 (double)((int)(pow(10., mf.mmin + (i + 1) * DELTAM) / params.ParticleMass) -
141 (int)(pow(10., mf.mmin + i * DELTAM) / params.ParticleMass));
142 if (dm > 0.0)
143 {
144 a = (double)mf.ninbin[i] / mf.vol / dm;
145 a1 = ((double)mf.ninbin[i] + sqrt((double)mf.ninbin[i])) / mf.vol / dm;
146 a2 = ((double)mf.ninbin[i] - sqrt((double)mf.ninbin[i])) / mf.vol / dm;
147 }
148 else
149 a = a1 = a2 = 0.0;
150
151 if (mf.ninbin[i] > 1)
152 mx = mf.massinbin[i] / (double)mf.ninbin[i];
153 else
154 mx = m;
155 a3 = AnalyticMassFunction(mx, outputs.z[iout]);
156
157 r = SizeForMass(mx);
158 massvar = MassVariance(r) * D * D;
159 sigma = sqrt(massvar);
160 ni = 1.686 / sigma;
161
162 fprintf(file,
163 " %15.8g %15.8g %15.8g %15.8g %10d %15.8g %15.8g\n",
164 mx * mf.hfactor,
165 a / mf.hfactor4,
166 a1 / mf.hfactor4,
167 a2 / mf.hfactor4,
168 mf.ninbin[i],
169 a3 / mf.hfactor4,
170 ni);
171 }
172 fclose(file);
173 }
174
175 // LEVARE
176 /* if (iout==1) */
177 /* { */
178 /* FILE *ff=fopen("mydump","w"); */
179 /* for (int u=0; u<subbox.Nstored; u++) */
180 /* #ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS */
181 /* fprintf(ff," %d %d %d %f %f %f\n", */
182 /* u,frag_pos[u],sorted_pos[u],frag[u].Fmax,frag[u].Vel[0],frag[u].Vel_prev[0]); */
183 /* #else */
184 /* fprintf(ff," %d %d %d %f %f %f\n", */
185 /* u,frag_pos[u],sorted_pos[u],frag[u].Fmax,frag[u].Vel[0],0.0); */
186 /* #endif */
187 /* fclose(ff); */
188 /* } */
189
190 /* Bye! */
191 return 0;
192}
193
194/*
195 GENERAL COMMUNICATION SCHEME
196
197 The code wants to write a catalog splitted in params.NumFiles
198 Each file will be written by this number of tasks
199 NTasksPerFile=NTasks/params.NumFiles;
200
201 Task ThisTask will write on file
202 ThisFile=ThisTask/NTasksPerFile;
203
204 Among the tasks that access the file, one task will collect information
205 and write it on the file. This is:
206 collector=ThisFile*NTasksPerFile;
207
208 1: initialization of whatever
209
210 2: each task constructs its own catalog (whatever it is)
211
212 3: collector task opens the file and writes the header
213
214 4: collector task writes the catalog on the file
215
216 5: loop on all other tasks that write on the same file
217
218 5a: the task sends its catalog to collector task
219 5b: collector task receives the catalog
220
221 5c: collector task writes the catalog on the file
222
223 6: collector task closes the file
224
225*/
226
227int write_catalog(int iout)
228{
229 /* Writes the group catalogues */
230
231 int igood, i, ngood, nhalos, j;
232 double hfactor, GGrid[3], SGrid[3];
233 char filename[LBLENGTH], labh[3];
234 int NTasksPerFile, collector, itask, next, ThisFile;
235 catalog_data *mycat;
236 FILE *file;
237 MPI_Status status;
238 int idummy;
239 pos_data obj1;
240
241 /* ordering of coordinates to accomodate for rotation caused by fft ordering */
242
243 if (params.DoNotWriteCatalogs)
244 {
245 if (!ThisTask)
246 printf("Halo catalog at z=%f will not be written\n", outputs.z[iout]);
247 return 0;
248 }
249
250 GGrid[0] = (double)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_];
251 GGrid[1] = (double)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_];
252 GGrid[2] = (double)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_];
253 SGrid[0] = (double)subbox.stabl[_x_];
254 SGrid[1] = (double)subbox.stabl[_y_];
255 SGrid[2] = (double)subbox.stabl[_z_];
256
257 NTasksPerFile = NTasks / params.NumFiles;
258 ThisFile = ThisTask / NTasksPerFile;
259 collector = ThisFile * NTasksPerFile;
260
261 /* output in H100 or in Htrue */
262 if (params.OutputInH100)
263 hfactor = params.Hubble100;
264 else
265 hfactor = 1.0;
266
267 /* each processor builds the catalogue */
268 nhalos = 0;
269 for (i = FILAMENT + 1, ngood = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
270 if (groups[i].point >= 0 && groups[i].good &&
271 groups[i].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
272 ngood++;
273
274 /* space to store catalogs */
275 mycat = (catalog_data *)wheretoplace_mycat;
276 if (ngood * sizeof(catalog_data) > subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(histories_data))
277 {
278 printf("ERROR on task %d: surprisingly, memory reserved to mycat is insufficient in write_catalog\n", ThisTask);
279 fflush(stdout);
280 return 1;
281 }
282
283 if (ngood)
284 {
285 for (i = FILAMENT + 1, igood = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
286 if (groups[i].point >= 0 && groups[i].good &&
287 groups[i].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
288 {
289 set_obj(i, outputs.F[iout], &obj1);
290 set_obj_vel(i, outputs.F[iout], &obj1);
291 mycat[igood].name = groups[i].name;
292#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
293 mycat[igood].n = groups[i].Mass;
294#endif
295 mycat[igood].M = groups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass * hfactor;
296 for (j = 0; j < 3; j++)
297 {
298 /* q and x are in sub-box coordinates, they are transformed
299 to global box coordinates (forcing PBCs) */
300 mycat[igood].q[j] = groups[i].Pos[j] + SGrid[j];
301 if (mycat[igood].q[j] < 0)
302 mycat[igood].q[j] += GGrid[j];
303 if (mycat[igood].q[j] >= GGrid[j])
304 mycat[igood].q[j] -= GGrid[j];
305 mycat[igood].q[j] *= params.InterPartDist * hfactor;
306
307 /* displacement is done up to ORDER_FOR_CATALOG */
308 mycat[igood].x[j] = q2x(j, &obj1, subbox.pbc[j], (double)subbox.Lgwbl[j], ORDER_FOR_CATALOG) + SGrid[j];
309 if (mycat[igood].x[j] < 0)
310 mycat[igood].x[j] += GGrid[j];
311 if (mycat[igood].x[j] >= GGrid[j])
312 mycat[igood].x[j] -= GGrid[j];
313 mycat[igood].x[j] *= params.InterPartDist * hfactor;
314 mycat[igood].v[j] = vel(j, &obj1);
315 }
316 igood++;
317 }
318 }
319
320 /* The collector task opens the file and writes its catalogue */
321 if (ThisTask == collector)
322 {
323 if (params.NumFiles > 1)
324 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.catalog.out.%d",
325 outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag, ThisFile);
326 else
327 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.catalog.out",
328 outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag);
329
330 if (!ThisTask)
331 printf("[%s] Opening file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
332
333 file = fopen(filename, "w");
334
335 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
336 {
337 fprintf(file, "# Group catalog for redshift %f and minimal mass of %d particle%s\n",
338 outputs.z[iout], params.MinHaloMass, (params.MinHaloMass == 1 ? "" : "s"));
339
340 if (params.OutputInH100)
341 strcpy(labh, "/h");
342 else
343 strcpy(labh, "");
344
345 fprintf(file, "# 1) group ID\n");
346 fprintf(file, "# 2) group mass (Msun%s)\n", labh);
347 fprintf(file, "# 3- 5) initial position (Mpc%s)\n", labh);
348 fprintf(file, "# 6- 8) final position (Mpc%s)\n", labh);
349 fprintf(file, "# 9-11) velocity (km/s)\n");
350#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
351 fprintf(file, "# 12) number of particles\n");
352#endif
353 fprintf(file, "#\n");
354
355 if (ngood)
356 for (igood = 0; igood < ngood; igood++)
357#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
358 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %13.6e %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %12d\n",
359#else
360 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %13.6e %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f\n",
361#endif
362 mycat[igood].name,
363 mycat[igood].M,
364 mycat[igood].q[0], mycat[igood].q[1], mycat[igood].q[2],
365 mycat[igood].x[0], mycat[igood].x[1], mycat[igood].x[2],
366 mycat[igood].v[0], mycat[igood].v[1], mycat[igood].v[2]
367#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
368 ,
369 mycat[igood].n
370#endif
371 );
372 }
373 else
374 {
375 idummy = 2 * sizeof(int);
376 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
377 fwrite(&NTasksPerFile, sizeof(int), 1, file);
378 idummy = sizeof(catalog_data);
379 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
380 idummy = 2 * sizeof(int);
381 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
382
383 idummy = sizeof(int);
384 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
385 fwrite(&ngood, sizeof(int), 1, file);
386 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
387
388 if (ngood)
389 {
390 idummy = ngood * sizeof(catalog_data);
391 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
392 for (igood = 0; igood < ngood; igood++)
393 fwrite(mycat + igood, sizeof(catalog_data), 1, file);
394 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
395 }
396 }
397
398 nhalos += ngood;
399 }
400
401 /* loop on other tasks that must give data to collector
402
403 itask sends the number of good groups, collector receives it
404 then itask sends the groups to collector (and deallocates mycat)
405 while collector (allocates mycat and) receives the groups;
406 finally collector writes mycat on the file
407 */
408 for (next = 1; next < NTasksPerFile; next++)
409 {
410 itask = collector + next;
411
412 if (ThisTask == collector)
413 {
414 ngood = 0;
415 MPI_Recv(&ngood, 1, MPI_INT, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
416
417 if (!params.CatalogInAscii)
418 {
419 idummy = sizeof(int);
420 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
421 fwrite(&ngood, sizeof(int), 1, file);
422 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
423 }
424
425 if (ngood)
426 {
427 MPI_Recv(mycat, ngood * sizeof(catalog_data), MPI_CHAR, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
428
429 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
430 {
431 for (igood = 0; igood < ngood; igood++)
432#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
433 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %13.6e %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %12d\n",
434#else
435 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %13.6e %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f %10.2f\n",
436#endif
437 mycat[igood].name,
438 mycat[igood].M,
439 mycat[igood].q[0], mycat[igood].q[1], mycat[igood].q[2],
440 mycat[igood].x[0], mycat[igood].x[1], mycat[igood].x[2],
441 mycat[igood].v[0], mycat[igood].v[1], mycat[igood].v[2]
442#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
443 ,
444 mycat[igood].n
445#endif
446 );
447 }
448 else
449 {
450 idummy = ngood * sizeof(catalog_data);
451 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
452 for (igood = 0; igood < ngood; igood++)
453 fwrite(mycat + igood, sizeof(catalog_data), 1, file);
454 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
455 }
456
457 nhalos += ngood;
458 }
459 }
460 else if (ThisTask == itask)
461 {
462 MPI_Send(&ngood, 1, MPI_INT, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
463 if (ngood)
464 {
465 MPI_Send(mycat, ngood * sizeof(catalog_data), MPI_CHAR, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
466 }
467 }
468 }
469 /* end of loop on tasks */
470 if (ThisTask == collector)
471 {
472 fclose(file);
473 printf("[%s] Task %d has written %d halos on file %s\n", fdate(), ThisTask, nhalos, filename);
474 fflush(stdout);
475 }
476
477 return 0;
478}
479
480#ifdef PLC
481int write_PLC(int flag)
482{
483 /* Writes the plc catalogues */
484
485 int i, nhalos, nstored;
486 double hfactor, rhor, theta, phi, v_los;
487 char filename[LBLENGTH], labh[3];
488 int NTasksPerFile, collector, itask, next, ThisFile;
489 FILE *file;
490 MPI_Status status;
491 static int FirstCall = 1;
492 plc_write_data writethis;
493 int idummy;
494
495 if (params.DoNotWriteCatalogs)
496 {
497 if (!ThisTask && FirstCall)
498 printf("PLC catalog will not be written\n");
499 return 0;
500 }
501
502 NTasksPerFile = NTasks / params.NumFiles;
503 ThisFile = ThisTask / NTasksPerFile;
504 collector = ThisFile * NTasksPerFile;
505
506 /* output in H100 or in Htrue */
507 if (params.OutputInH100)
508 hfactor = params.Hubble100;
509 else
510 hfactor = 1.0;
511 nhalos = 0;
512
513 /* The collector task opens the file and writes its catalogue */
514 if (ThisTask == collector)
515 {
516 if (params.NumFiles > 1)
517 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.plc.out.%d", params.RunFlag, ThisFile);
518 else
519 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.plc.out", params.RunFlag);
520
521 if (!ThisTask)
522 {
523 if (FirstCall)
524 printf("[%s] Opening file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
525 else
526 printf("[%s] Updating file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
527 }
528
529 if (FirstCall)
530 file = fopen(filename, "w");
531 else
532 file = fopen(filename, "a");
533
534 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
535 {
536 if (FirstCall)
537 {
538 fprintf(file, "# Group catalog on the Past Light Cone for a minimal mass of %d particle%s\n",
539 params.MinHaloMass, (params.MinHaloMass == 1 ? "" : "s"));
540
541 if (params.OutputInH100)
542 strcpy(labh, "/h");
543 else
544 strcpy(labh, "");
545
546#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
547 fprintf(file, "# 1) group ID\n");
548 fprintf(file, "# 2) true redshift\n");
549 fprintf(file, "# 3-5) comoving position (Mpc%s)\n", labh);
550 fprintf(file, "# 6-8) velocity (km/s)\n");
551 fprintf(file, "# 9) group mass (Msun%s)\n", labh);
552 fprintf(file, "# 10) theta (degree)\n");
553 fprintf(file, "# 11) phi (degree)\n");
554 fprintf(file, "# 12) peculiar velocity along the line-of-sight (km/s)\n");
555 fprintf(file, "# 13) observed redshift\n");
556#else
557 fprintf(file, "# 1) group ID\n");
558 fprintf(file, "# 2) true redshift\n");
559 fprintf(file, "# 3) group mass (Msun%s)\n", labh);
560 fprintf(file, "# 4) theta (degree)\n");
561 fprintf(file, "# 5) phi (degree)\n");
562 fprintf(file, "# 6) observed redshift\n");
563#endif
564 fprintf(file, "#\n");
565 }
566
567 for (i = 0; i < plc.Nstored; i++)
568 {
569 coord_transformation_cartesian_polar(plcgroups[i].x, &rhor, &theta, &phi);
570 writethis.name = plcgroups[i].name;
571 writethis.red = plcgroups[i].z;
572 writethis.Mass = plcgroups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass * hfactor;
573 writethis.theta = theta;
574 writethis.phi = phi;
575 v_los = (plcgroups[i].x[0] * plcgroups[i].v[0] +
576 plcgroups[i].x[1] * plcgroups[i].v[1] +
577 plcgroups[i].x[2] * plcgroups[i].v[2]) /
578 rhor;
579 writethis.obsz = plcgroups[i].z + v_los / SPEEDOFLIGHT * (1.0 + plcgroups[i].z);
580#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
581 writethis.vx = plcgroups[i].v[0];
582 writethis.vy = plcgroups[i].v[1];
583 writethis.vz = plcgroups[i].v[2];
584 writethis.x = plcgroups[i].x[0] * hfactor;
585 writethis.y = plcgroups[i].x[1] * hfactor;
586 writethis.z = plcgroups[i].x[2] * hfactor;
587 writethis.v_los = v_los;
588#endif
589
590#ifdef LIGHT_OUTPUT
591 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %16.6f %15.8e %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f\n",
592 writethis.name,
593 writethis.red,
594 writethis.Mass,
595 writethis.theta,
596 writethis.phi,
597 writethis.obsz);
598#else
599 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %15.8e %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f\n",
600 writethis.name,
601 writethis.red,
602 writethis.x, writethis.y, writethis.z,
603 writethis.vx, writethis.vy, writethis.vz,
604 writethis.Mass,
605 writethis.theta,
606 writethis.phi,
607 writethis.v_los,
608 writethis.obsz);
609#endif
610 nhalos++;
611 }
612 }
613 else
614 {
615
616 if (FirstCall)
617 {
618 idummy = sizeof(int);
619 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
620 idummy = sizeof(plc_write_data);
621 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
622 idummy = sizeof(int);
623 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
624 }
625
626 idummy = sizeof(int);
627 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
628 fwrite(&plc.Nstored, sizeof(int), 1, file);
629 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
630
631 idummy = plc.Nstored * sizeof(plc_write_data);
632 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
633
634 for (i = 0; i < plc.Nstored; i++)
635 {
636 coord_transformation_cartesian_polar(plcgroups[i].x, &rhor, &theta, &phi);
637 writethis.name = plcgroups[i].name;
638 writethis.red = plcgroups[i].z;
639 writethis.Mass = plcgroups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass * hfactor;
640 writethis.theta = theta;
641 writethis.phi = phi;
642 v_los = (plcgroups[i].x[0] * plcgroups[i].v[0] +
643 plcgroups[i].x[1] * plcgroups[i].v[1] +
644 plcgroups[i].x[2] * plcgroups[i].v[2]) /
645 rhor;
646 writethis.obsz = plcgroups[i].z + v_los / SPEEDOFLIGHT * (1.0 + plcgroups[i].z);
647#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
648 writethis.vx = plcgroups[i].v[0];
649 writethis.vy = plcgroups[i].v[1];
650 writethis.vz = plcgroups[i].v[2];
651 writethis.x = plcgroups[i].x[0] * hfactor;
652 writethis.y = plcgroups[i].x[1] * hfactor;
653 writethis.z = plcgroups[i].x[2] * hfactor;
654 writethis.v_los = v_los;
655#endif
656
657 idummy = sizeof(plc_write_data);
658 fwrite(&writethis, sizeof(plc_write_data), 1, file);
659 nhalos++;
660 }
661 idummy = plc.Nstored * sizeof(plc_write_data);
662 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
663 }
664 }
665
666 /* loop on other tasks that collect data on collector
667
668 itask sends the number of good groups, collector receives it
669 then itask sends the groups to collector (and deallocates mycat)
670 while collector (allocates mycat and) receives the groups;
671 finally collector writes mycat on the file
672 */
673 for (next = 1; next < NTasksPerFile; next++)
674 {
675 itask = collector + next;
676
677 if (ThisTask == collector)
678 {
679 nstored = 0;
680 MPI_Recv(&nstored, 1, MPI_INT, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
681
682 if (nstored)
683 {
684
685 MPI_Recv(plcgroups, nstored * sizeof(plcgroup_data), MPI_CHAR, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
686
687 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
688 {
689 for (i = 0; i < nstored; i++)
690 {
691 coord_transformation_cartesian_polar(plcgroups[i].x, &rhor, &theta, &phi);
692 writethis.name = plcgroups[i].name;
693 writethis.red = plcgroups[i].z;
694 writethis.Mass = plcgroups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass * hfactor;
695 writethis.theta = theta;
696 writethis.phi = phi;
697 v_los = (plcgroups[i].x[0] * plcgroups[i].v[0] +
698 plcgroups[i].x[1] * plcgroups[i].v[1] +
699 plcgroups[i].x[2] * plcgroups[i].v[2]) /
700 rhor;
701 writethis.obsz = plcgroups[i].z + v_los / SPEEDOFLIGHT * (1.0 + plcgroups[i].z);
702#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
703 writethis.vx = plcgroups[i].v[0];
704 writethis.vy = plcgroups[i].v[1];
705 writethis.vz = plcgroups[i].v[2];
706 writethis.x = plcgroups[i].x[0] * hfactor;
707 writethis.y = plcgroups[i].x[1] * hfactor;
708 writethis.z = plcgroups[i].x[2] * hfactor;
709 writethis.v_los = v_los;
710#endif
711
712#ifdef LIGHT_OUTPUT
713 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %16.6f %15.8e %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f\n",
714 writethis.name,
715 writethis.red,
716 writethis.Mass,
717 writethis.theta,
718 writethis.phi,
719 writethis.obsz);
720#else
721 fprintf(file, " %12Lu %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %15.8e %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f %16.6f\n",
722 writethis.name,
723 writethis.red,
724 writethis.x, writethis.y, writethis.z,
725 writethis.vx, writethis.vy, writethis.vz,
726 writethis.Mass,
727 writethis.theta,
728 writethis.phi,
729 writethis.v_los,
730 writethis.obsz);
731#endif
732 nhalos++;
733 }
734 }
735 else
736 {
737 idummy = sizeof(int);
738 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
739 fwrite(&nstored, sizeof(int), 1, file);
740 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
741
742 idummy = nstored * sizeof(plc_write_data);
743 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
744 for (i = 0; i < nstored; i++)
745 {
746 coord_transformation_cartesian_polar(plcgroups[i].x, &rhor, &theta, &phi);
747 writethis.name = plcgroups[i].name;
748 writethis.red = plcgroups[i].z;
749 writethis.Mass = plcgroups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass * hfactor;
750 writethis.theta = theta;
751 writethis.phi = phi;
752 v_los = (plcgroups[i].x[0] * plcgroups[i].v[0] +
753 plcgroups[i].x[1] * plcgroups[i].v[1] +
754 plcgroups[i].x[2] * plcgroups[i].v[2]) /
755 rhor;
756 writethis.obsz = plcgroups[i].z + v_los / SPEEDOFLIGHT * (1.0 + plcgroups[i].z);
757#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
758 writethis.vx = plcgroups[i].v[0];
759 writethis.vy = plcgroups[i].v[1];
760 writethis.vz = plcgroups[i].v[2];
761 writethis.x = plcgroups[i].x[0] * hfactor;
762 writethis.y = plcgroups[i].x[1] * hfactor;
763 writethis.z = plcgroups[i].x[2] * hfactor;
764 writethis.v_los = v_los;
765#endif
766
767 fwrite(&writethis, sizeof(plc_write_data), 1, file);
768
769 nhalos++;
770 }
771 idummy = nstored * sizeof(plc_write_data);
772 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
773 }
774 }
775 }
776 else if (ThisTask == itask)
777 {
778 MPI_Send(&plc.Nstored, 1, MPI_INT, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
779 if (plc.Nstored)
780 {
781 MPI_Send(plcgroups, plc.Nstored * sizeof(plcgroup_data), MPI_CHAR, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
782 }
783 }
784 }
785 /* end of loop on tasks */
786 if (ThisTask == collector)
787 {
788 fclose(file);
789 printf("[%s] Task %d has written %d halos on file %s\n", fdate(), ThisTask, nhalos, filename);
790 fflush(stdout);
791 }
792
793 FirstCall = 0;
794
795 /* writes the n(z) at the last call */
796 if (flag)
797 {
798 /* Guard against invalid or inconsistent bin counts, and synchronize them across ranks */
799 int nzbins_local = plc.nzbins;
800 if (nzbins_local < 0)
801 {
802 fprintf(stderr, "[Task %d] ERROR: plc.nzbins is negative (%d) in write_halos.\n", ThisTask, nzbins_local);
803 fflush(stderr);
804 MPI_Abort(MPI_COMM_WORLD, 99);
805 }
806 int nz_min = 0, nz_max = 0;
807 MPI_Allreduce(&nzbins_local, &nz_min, 1, MPI_INT, MPI_MIN, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
808 MPI_Allreduce(&nzbins_local, &nz_max, 1, MPI_INT, MPI_MAX, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
809 if (nz_min != nz_max)
810 {
811 fprintf(stderr, "[Task %d] ERROR: Inconsistent plc.nzbins across ranks (min=%d, max=%d). Aborting to avoid MPI_Reduce mismatch.\n", ThisTask, nz_min, nz_max);
812 fflush(stderr);
813 MPI_Abort(MPI_COMM_WORLD, 99);
814 }
815 int nzbins = nz_min; /* synchronized count for all ranks */
816 double *totnz = (nzbins > 0) ? (double *)calloc(nzbins, sizeof(double)) : NULL;
817
818 /* Only perform reduction if there is at least one bin */
819 if (nzbins > 0)
820 MPI_Reduce(plc.nz, totnz, nzbins, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
821 else
822 MPI_Reduce(NULL, NULL, 0, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
823
824 /* Task 0 writes the file */
825 if (!ThisTask)
826 {
827 double tot = 0;
828 for (int ibin = 0; ibin < nzbins; ibin++)
829 tot += totnz[ibin];
830 printf("[%s] total number of halos in the PLC: %g\n", fdate(), tot);
831
832 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.nz.out", params.RunFlag);
833 printf("[%s] Opening file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
834 file = fopen(filename, "w");
835 fprintf(file, "# 1) lower z for bin\n");
836 fprintf(file, "# 2) upper z for bin\n");
837 fprintf(file, "# 3) number of objects in bin\n");
838 fprintf(file, "# 4) number per square degree\n");
839 fprintf(file, "# 5) prediction for number of objects in bin\n");
840 fprintf(file, "#\n");
841
842 double skyarea = (1 - cos((params.PLCAperture > 90. ? 90. : params.PLCAperture) / 180. * PI)) * 2. * PI * pow(180. / PI, 2.);
843 for (int ibin = 0; ibin < nzbins; ibin++)
844 {
845 double zlow = params.LastzForPLC + ibin * plc.delta_z;
846 double zhigh = params.LastzForPLC + (ibin + 1) * plc.delta_z;
847 if (ibin == nzbins - 1)
848 zhigh = params.StartingzForPLC;
849 double prediction = compute_Nhalos_in_PLC(zlow, zhigh);
850 fprintf(file, "%8.3f %8.3f %12g %8.3f %12g\n",
851 zlow, zhigh, totnz[ibin], totnz[ibin] / skyarea, prediction);
852 }
853
854 fclose(file);
855 }
856 if (totnz)
857 free(totnz);
858 }
859
860 return 0;
861}
862#endif
863
864#ifdef ONLYFORFIRSTBHS
865int write_histories(int iout)
866#else
867int write_histories(void)
868#endif
869{
870 /* writes merger histories at the end of the run */
871
872 int i, commint[2], ntrees, nbranch_all, nbranch_tree, ntrees_global, nbranch_global,
873 ibranch, thisbranch, thistree, itree;
874 char filename[LBLENGTH];
875 int NTasksPerFile, collector, itask, next, ThisFile;
876 histories_data *mycat;
877 FILE *file;
878 MPI_Status status;
879 int idummy;
880
881 if (params.DoNotWriteHistories)
882 {
883 if (!ThisTask)
884 printf("Merger histories will not be written\n");
885 return 0;
886 }
887
888 NTasksPerFile = NTasks / params.NumFiles;
889 ThisFile = ThisTask / NTasksPerFile;
890 collector = ThisFile * NTasksPerFile;
891
892 for (i = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
893 groups[i].trackT = 0;
894 for (i = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
895 groups[i].trackC = 0;
896
897 /* each processor builds its catalogue */
898
899 /* this loop counts the total number of branches in the local catalog */
900 nbranch_all = ntrees = 0;
901 for (i = FILAMENT + 1, nbranch_all = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
902 if (groups[groups[i].halo_app].good && /* if the halo belongs to a good group */
903 groups[groups[i].halo_app].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass) /* and if its mass is large enough */
904 nbranch_all++;
905
906 mycat = (histories_data *)wheretoplace_mycat;
907 if (nbranch_all * sizeof(histories_data) > subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(histories_data))
908 {
909 printf("ERROR on task %d: surprisingly, memory reserved to mycat is insufficient in write_histories\n", ThisTask);
910 fflush(stdout);
911 return 1;
912 }
913
914 /* assign the catalog by looping on all the trees */
915 if (nbranch_all)
916 {
917 ntrees = 0; /* number of trees (of main halos at the final redshift) */
918 thisbranch = 0; /* this is the counter of the number of stored halos (branches) */
919
920 /* loop on main halos */
921 for (i = FILAMENT + 1, ntrees = 0; i <= ngroups; i++)
922 if (groups[i].halo_app == i && /* if this is a main halo */
923 groups[i].good && /* and if it is a good one */
924 groups[i].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass) /* and if its mass is large enough */
925 {
926 ++ntrees;
927
928 /* counts the number of branches in the tree */
929 nbranch_tree = 0;
930 next = i;
931 do
932 {
933 ++nbranch_tree;
934 next = groups[next].ll; /* next branch in the tree */
935 } while (next != i);
936
937 ibranch = 0; /* this is the counter of branches within the tree, starting from zero
938 but zero is assigned to the main halo, whose nick will be nbranch_tree */
939 next = i; /* this is the pointer to the halo in the main catalog */
940 /* NB: we start from the main halo */
941
942 do
943 {
944 /* track vector keeps track of the relation between main catalog and numbering within the single tree */
945 groups[thisbranch].trackT = next;
946 mycat[thisbranch].nick = groups[next].trackC = (ibranch ? ibranch : nbranch_tree);
947 mycat[thisbranch].ll = ++ibranch;
948 mycat[thisbranch].mass = groups[next].Mass;
949 mycat[thisbranch].name = groups[next].name;
950 mycat[thisbranch].mam = groups[next].mass_at_merger;
951 mycat[thisbranch].zap = FTOZ(groups[next].t_appear);
952 mycat[thisbranch].zpe = FTOZ(groups[next].t_peak);
953 mycat[thisbranch].zme = FTOZ(groups[next].t_merge);
954 ++thisbranch;
955
956 next = groups[next].ll; /* next branch in the tree */
957 } /* this loop closes when we get to the original halo, that is stored as the last one */
958 while (next != i);
959
960 /* now it fixes all the merged_with fields */
961 for (ibranch = thisbranch - nbranch_tree; ibranch < thisbranch; ibranch++)
962 if (groups[groups[ibranch].trackT].merged_with > FILAMENT)
963 mycat[ibranch].mw = groups[groups[groups[ibranch].trackT].merged_with].trackC;
964 else
965 mycat[ibranch].mw = -1;
966 }
967 }
968
969 /* computes the total number of trees and branches in the file */
970 /* NB: this could be done by a reduce, but redefining the communicator is boring */
971 if (ThisTask == collector)
972 {
973 ntrees_global = ntrees;
974 nbranch_global = nbranch_all;
975 }
976 for (next = 1; next < NTasksPerFile; next++)
977 {
978 itask = collector + next;
979 if (ThisTask == collector)
980 {
981 MPI_Recv(commint, 2, MPI_INT, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
982 ntrees_global += commint[0];
983 nbranch_global += commint[1];
984 }
985 else if (ThisTask == itask)
986 {
987 commint[0] = ntrees;
988 commint[1] = nbranch_all;
989 MPI_Send(commint, 2, MPI_INT, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
990 }
991 }
992
993 /* collector task opens the file and writes the header */
994 if (ThisTask == collector)
995 {
996
997#ifdef ONLYFORFIRSTBHS
998 if (params.NumFiles > 1)
999 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.histories.out.%d", outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag, ThisFile);
1000 else
1001 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.histories.out", outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag);
1002#else
1003 if (params.NumFiles > 1)
1004 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.histories.out.%d", params.RunFlag, ThisFile);
1005 else
1006 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.histories.out", params.RunFlag);
1007#endif
1008
1009 if (!ThisTask)
1010 printf("[%s] Opening file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
1011
1012 file = fopen(filename, "w");
1013
1014 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
1015 {
1016 fprintf(file, "# Merger histories for halos with minimal mass of %d particle%s\n",
1017 params.MinHaloMass, (params.MinHaloMass == 1 ? "" : "s"));
1018
1019 fprintf(file, "# 1) group ID\n");
1020 fprintf(file, "# 2) index within the tree\n");
1021 fprintf(file, "# 3) linking list\n");
1022 fprintf(file, "# 4) merged with\n");
1023 fprintf(file, "# 5) mass of halo at merger (particles)\n");
1024 fprintf(file, "# 6) mass of main halo it merges with, at merger (particles)\n");
1025 fprintf(file, "# 7) merger redshift\n");
1026 fprintf(file, "# 8) redshift of peak collapse\n");
1027 fprintf(file, "# 9) redshift at which the halo overtakes the minimal mass\n");
1028 fprintf(file, "#\n");
1029 fprintf(file, "# Ntrees & Nbranches: \n");
1030 fprintf(file, " %d %d\n", ntrees_global, nbranch_global);
1031 thistree = 0;
1032 }
1033 else
1034 {
1035 idummy = sizeof(int);
1036 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1037 idummy = sizeof(histories_data);
1038 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1039 idummy = sizeof(int);
1040 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1041
1042 idummy = 2 * sizeof(int);
1043 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1044 fwrite(&ntrees_global, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1045 fwrite(&nbranch_global, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1046 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1047 }
1048 }
1049
1050 /* collector writes its catalog */
1051 if (ThisTask == collector)
1052 {
1053 if (nbranch_all)
1054 {
1055 thisbranch = 0;
1056 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
1057 {
1058 for (itree = 0; itree < ntrees; itree++)
1059 {
1060 nbranch_tree = mycat[thisbranch].nick;
1061 fprintf(file, "#Tree %d, Nbranches=%d\n", thistree++, nbranch_tree);
1062 for (ibranch = 0; ibranch < nbranch_tree; ibranch++)
1063 {
1064 fprintf(file, " %12Ld %6d %6d %6d %9d %9d %9.4f %9.4f %9.4f\n",
1065 mycat[thisbranch].name,
1066 mycat[thisbranch].nick,
1067 mycat[thisbranch].ll,
1068 mycat[thisbranch].mw,
1069 mycat[thisbranch].mass,
1070 mycat[thisbranch].mam,
1071 mycat[thisbranch].zme,
1072 mycat[thisbranch].zpe,
1073 mycat[thisbranch].zap);
1074 ++thisbranch;
1075 }
1076 }
1077 }
1078 else
1079 {
1080
1081#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
1082 idummy = sizeof(int);
1083 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1084 fwrite(&ntrees, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1085 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1086
1087 idummy = ntrees * sizeof(int);
1088 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1089 for (itree = 0, ibranch = 0; itree < ntrees; itree++)
1090 {
1091 fwrite(&mycat[ibranch].nick, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1092 ibranch += mycat[ibranch].nick;
1093 }
1094 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1095
1096 idummy = nbranch_all * sizeof(histories_data);
1097 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1098#endif
1099 for (ibranch = 0; ibranch < nbranch_all; ibranch++)
1100 fwrite(mycat + ibranch, sizeof(histories_data), 1, file);
1101#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
1102 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1103#endif
1104
1105 /*
1106 nbranch_tree=mycat[thisbranch].nick;
1107 idummy=2*sizeof(int);
1108 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1109 fwrite(&thistree,sizeof(int),1,file);
1110 fwrite(&nbranch_tree,sizeof(int),1,file);
1111 ++thistree;
1112 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1113
1114 idummy=nbranch_tree*sizeof(histories_data);
1115 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1116 for (ibranch=0; ibranch<nbranch_tree; ibranch++)
1117 fwrite(mycat+thisbranch++,sizeof(histories_data),1,file);
1118 idummy=nbranch_tree*sizeof(histories_data);
1119 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1120 } */
1121 }
1122 }
1123 }
1124
1125 /* loop on other tasks that must give data to collector
1126
1127 itask sends the number of good groups, collector receives it
1128 then itask sends the groups to collector (and deallocates mycat)
1129 while collector (allocates mycat and) receives the groups;
1130 finally collector writes mycat on the file
1131 */
1132
1133 for (next = 1; next < NTasksPerFile; next++)
1134 {
1135 itask = collector + next;
1136
1137 if (ThisTask == collector)
1138 {
1139 MPI_Recv(commint, 2, MPI_INT, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
1140 nbranch_all = commint[0];
1141 ntrees = commint[1];
1142
1143 if (nbranch_all)
1144 {
1145 MPI_Recv(mycat, nbranch_all * sizeof(histories_data), MPI_CHAR, itask, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
1146
1147 /* writes the catalog that has just received */
1148 thisbranch = 0;
1149 if (params.CatalogInAscii)
1150 {
1151 for (itree = 0; itree < ntrees; itree++)
1152 {
1153 nbranch_tree = mycat[thisbranch].nick;
1154 fprintf(file, "#Tree %d, Nbranches=%d\n", thistree++, nbranch_tree);
1155 for (ibranch = 0; ibranch < nbranch_tree; ibranch++)
1156 {
1157 fprintf(file, " %12Ld %6d %6d %6d %9d %9d %9.4f %9.4f %9.4f\n",
1158 mycat[thisbranch].name,
1159 mycat[thisbranch].nick,
1160 mycat[thisbranch].ll,
1161 mycat[thisbranch].mw,
1162 mycat[thisbranch].mass,
1163 mycat[thisbranch].mam,
1164 mycat[thisbranch].zme,
1165 mycat[thisbranch].zpe,
1166 mycat[thisbranch].zap);
1167 ++thisbranch;
1168 }
1169 }
1170 }
1171 else
1172 {
1173#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
1174 idummy = sizeof(int);
1175 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1176 fwrite(&ntrees, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1177 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1178
1179 idummy = ntrees * sizeof(int);
1180 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1181 for (itree = 0, ibranch = 0; itree < ntrees; itree++)
1182 {
1183 fwrite(&mycat[ibranch].nick, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1184 ibranch += mycat[ibranch].nick;
1185 }
1186 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1187
1188 idummy = nbranch_all * sizeof(histories_data);
1189 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1190#endif
1191 for (ibranch = 0; ibranch < nbranch_all; ibranch++)
1192 fwrite(mycat + ibranch, sizeof(histories_data), 1, file);
1193#ifndef LIGHT_OUTPUT
1194 fwrite(&idummy, sizeof(int), 1, file);
1195#endif
1196
1197 /*
1198 for (itree=0; itree<ntrees; itree++)
1199 {
1200 nbranch_tree=mycat[thisbranch].nick;
1201 idummy=2*sizeof(int);
1202 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1203 fwrite(&thistree,sizeof(int),1,file);
1204 fwrite(&nbranch_tree,sizeof(int),1,file);
1205 ++thistree;
1206 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1207
1208 idummy=nbranch_tree*sizeof(histories_data);
1209 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1210 for (ibranch=0; ibranch<nbranch_tree; ibranch++)
1211 fwrite(mycat+thisbranch++,sizeof(histories_data),1,file);
1212 idummy=nbranch_tree*sizeof(histories_data);
1213 fwrite(&idummy,sizeof(int),1,file);
1214 } */
1215 }
1216 }
1217 }
1218 else if (ThisTask == itask)
1219 {
1220 /* itask sends its data to collector */
1221 commint[0] = nbranch_all;
1222 commint[1] = ntrees;
1223 MPI_Send(commint, 2, MPI_INT, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
1224
1225 if (nbranch_all)
1226 {
1227 MPI_Send(mycat, nbranch_all * sizeof(histories_data), MPI_CHAR, collector, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
1228 }
1229 }
1230 }
1231
1232 /* end of loop on tasks */
1233 if (ThisTask == collector)
1234 {
1235 fclose(file);
1236 printf("[%s] Task %d has written %d trees and %d branches on file %s\n", fdate(), ThisTask, ntrees_global, nbranch_global, filename);
1237 fflush(stdout);
1238 }
1239
1240 return 0;
1241}
vel
PRODFLOAT vel(int i, pos_data *myobj)
